params.text:
Communication and de-escalation techniques

De-escalation is the set of skills you use to reduce agitation and help a person regain control. It depends far more on how you communicate than on what you say. A calm, respectful presence often does more than any single phrase.

  • Stay calm — control your own tone and breathing; your composure is contagious, and so is panic.
  • Listen actively — give the person your full attention and let them express their concern.
  • Show empathy — acknowledge how they feel; feeling heard reduces the need to escalate.
  • Use non-threatening body language — relaxed posture, open hands, avoid staring or squaring up.
  • Give space — keep a respectful distance so the person does not feel cornered or crowded.
  • Offer choices — restoring a sense of control helps a person feel less powerless.
  • Do not argue — avoid correcting, blaming, or trying to win; that fuels the conflict.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete these de-escalation principles.
params.textField: Staying *calm* helps to defuse tension, because panic is contagious. Listen *actively* so the person feels genuinely heard. Show *empathy* by acknowledging how they feel. Give the person *space* so they do not feel cornered. Offer *choices* to restore their sense of control. Above all, do not *argue*, as trying to win only fuels the conflict.
